chunks.text: Media Mentions Archive - Page 2 of 5 - EECS at Berkeley. June 12, 2025: May the Best Bot Win. Started as student side project, Chatbot Arena is now the AI industry’s scoreboard—and a $100 million startup. May 5, 2025: SF Chronicle: One UC major leads to the highest paying jobs — even more than the same degree elsewhere.
chunks.question: What is Chatbot Arena and its significance?

chunks.text: March 31, 2025: WIRED: Angelina Jolie Was Right About Computers. David Patterson, professor emeritus of computer science, has reshaped modern computing with his groundbreaking work on RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) architecture. Wired explores how his early research at UC Berkeley laid the foundation for RISC-V, an open-source alternative now driving innovation across the industry.
chunks.question: Who contributed to RISC-V and how?

chunks.text: March 17, 2025: NPR: Researchers are rushing to build AI-powered robots. But will they work? EECS Professor Ken Goldberg and EECS Alumna Chelsea Finn (Ph.D. ’18) discuss the latest advancements in AI and their integration into robotics.
chunks.question: Who discussed AI-powered robots on NPR?

chunks.text: February 12, 2025: 20 UC Berkeley alumni, students and faculty shine on Forbes 30 Under 30 list. EECS alumni named in Forbes 30 Under 30 list. January 30, 2025: NBC News: Ion Stoica on DeepSeek. EECS Professor Ion Stoica discusses DeepSeek with Gadi Schwartz on NBC News NOW.
chunks.question: What recognition did UC Berkeley affiliates receive?

chunks.text: January 17, 2025: Ion Stoica featured on Sequoia Capital’s Training Data podcast. EECS Professor and Databricks co-founder Ion Stoica is featured on Sequoia Capital’s Training Data podcast. January 14, 2025: Ken Goldberg featured on Armchair Expert. EECS Professor Ken Goldberg is featured as a guest on Armchair Expert.
chunks.question: Where was Ion Stoica featured in January 2025?

chunks.text: December 6, 2024: The UC Berkeley Project That Is the AI Industry’s Obsession. EECS grad students Anastasios Angelopoulos (advisors: Jordan and Malik) and Wei-Lin Chiang (advisor: Stoica) were profiled by The Wall Street Journal for their work on Chatbot Arena.
chunks.question: Who was profiled for Chatbot Arena work?

chunks.text: November 25, 2024: Election Forecasts Try to Go Beyond the Polls. Are They Helping? UC Berkeley EECS Professor Benjamin Recht was recently featured in The New York Times for his critique of election forecasting models, emphasizing the complex decisions behind these predictions. His insights spotlight the intersection of statistical modeling and societal impact.
chunks.question: What did Benjamin Recht critique?
